stack: Pure static site: plain HTML/CSS/JS, no framework, no bundler, no npm, no build step — 42 HTML pages served as-is. One shared design system at styles/shared.css (~1,419 lines of CSS custom properties: base palette, 8 per-topic accent sets, type scale, 8px spacing scale, widget kit, easing tokens). Per-page interactivity lives in inline <style> and <script> blocks written as vanilla-JS IIFEs (IntersectionObserver for TOC highlighting/visibility, canvas for generative heroes, requestAnimationFrame progress bar) — no ES modules, no dependencies. Typography via Google Fonts (Newsreader serif for prose/display, Inter for UI, JetBrains Mono for code/labels — the 'Three Voices Rule' in DESIGN.md). Google Analytics gtag (G-E8RQLQXRWH) inlined in every page head. Tooling is stdlib-only Python 3 in scripts/: check_links.py (internal href/src resolver), check_scripts.py, inject_meta.py (idempotent OG/Twitter/favicon meta injection derived from each page's .lead paragraph), lint.py (writes wiki/LINT.md: topic balance, orphan concepts, broken cross-refs, stale articles >180 days, cross-topic overlap). There is also a committed wiki/ markdown planning layer (maps/, themes/, entities/, SCHEMA.md, AUDIENCE.md) used for editorial planning, excluded from the deployed link surface.
deploySetup: GitHub Pages classic branch deploy, no CI at all. Verified via `gh api repos/NavSha/interactive-explainers/pages`: build_type "legacy" (Jekyll-classic build), source = branch `master`, path `/`, custom_404: true (404.html at root), https_enforced: true, no custom domain (cname: null), served at https://navsha.github.io/interactive-explainers/. There is no .github/ directory — no Actions, no workflows; push to master auto-deploys in ~1 min (documented in CLAUDE.md). No .nojekyll file (safe because no underscore-prefixed dirs). Development flow is PR-based with typed topic branches (article/*, design/*, audit/*, design/*) merged into master via GitHub PRs. SEO surface is hand-maintained: sitemap.xml (priority tiers: 1.0 home, 0.8 series indexes, 0.7 articles), robots.txt pointing at the sitemap. All asset/CSS links are relative (../styles/shared.css) so the site works under the /interactive-explainers/ project-pages subpath; absolute URLs appear only in og:/twitter: meta and sitemap. Cache busting is manual: shared.css is linked as shared.css?v=N and the version must be bumped across every HTML file when the CSS changes (currently v=12; note CLAUDE.md still says v=10 — the doc drifts). The ml-papers repo currently has NO Pages site enabled (API returns 404) and its default branch is `main`.
conventions: Design-token CSS: everything derives from :root custom properties in styles/shared.css — base palette (warm paper #FAF8F5, ink #1A1A1A), per-topic accent quads (--accent-X, -deep, -light, -gradient), 8px spacing scale, 760px content/widget width, easing/duration tokens.
One voice, eight accents: each series gets an accent color + gradient + light wash; identity only, never semantics (no info by color alone).
Three Voices typography rule: Newsreader serif for prose/display, Inter sans for UI, JetBrains Mono for labels/code; loaded from Google Fonts with preconnect.

Article anatomy is rigid and repeatable: hero with canvas + breadcrumb (Site / Series / Part N) + 'Part N of M · ~X min read' meta → fixed left TOC sidebar (>=1280px, appears after hero via IntersectionObserver, Roman-numeral items) → 4-bar rap-verse epigraph (brand system, per PRODUCT.md) → <article class="prose"> with h2[id] + Roman section numbers → interactive .widget blocks between prose sections → quiz → <nav class="article-nav"> footer (back-to-series / next-part links) → reading progress bar.
Widgets use the .widget class system, must be keyboard-operable, read sensibly linearly, and have prefers-reduced-motion alternatives; accessibility floor is WCAG AA (body text >=4.5:1).
Every page carries full OG/Twitter/description meta + favicon links, injected idempotently by scripts/inject_meta.py (description pulled from the .lead paragraph).
CSS cache busting: shared.css?v=N, bump N in every HTML file when shared.css changes — the top 'Critical' warning in CLAUDE.md.
Stdlib-only Python maintenance scripts (no third-party deps, Python 3.9+): link checking, meta injection, editorial linting into wiki/LINT.md.

Governance docs trio: CLAUDE.md (how to work), PRODUCT.md (register, audience, brand personality, anti-references, 5 design principles), DESIGN.md (tokens, component specs, North Star 'The Builder's Field Guide') — design work must read PRODUCT.md/DESIGN.md first.

Git hygiene: typed branches (article/*, design/*, audit/*), PR merges to master, imperative scoped commit subjects ('Homepage: masthead tokenizer widget...'); local tooling dirs (.claude/, .impeccable/, .gstack/) gitignored.
Relative internal links everywhere so the site works under a GitHub project-pages subpath; absolute URLs only in meta tags and sitemap.xml.
Hand-maintained sitemap.xml with priority tiers and robots.txt; custom 404.html at root.
navAndIA: Hub-and-spoke, two levels deep, series-first. Level 0: index.html landing = masthead hero (generative token-field canvas, tokenizer tagline that is itself a widget) → topic-card grid of 8 series with per-topic accent colors and part counts (one 'wide card' variant to break the grid stamp) → 'latest' strip (series name + article title links) → follow banner (@navsha on X) → minimal site footer (Airtribe attribution). Level 1: each series is a directory (agents/, llm-fundamentals/, context-windows/, cost-latency/, grounding/, evaluation/, ai-ux/, vibe-coding/) with its own index.html series landing listing 2–5 parts. Level 2: article pages, sequenced as 'Part N of M', with breadcrumb up-navigation (Home / Series / Part), an in-page fixed TOC as the primary within-article nav, and an article-nav footer for prev/next/back-to-series. There is no global nav bar or cross-series sidebar — navigation is strictly hierarchical plus occasional in-prose cross-links between articles (which lint.py checks for 404s). Plus a standalone infographics/ section (3 shareable one-pagers) outside the series system. IA planning happens off-page in wiki/maps/<series>.md with parts_live/parts_planned frontmatter.
crossLinkOpportunities: Transformer / GPT-2 paper entries ↔ llm-fundamentals/ (how-llms-work.html, tokenization.html): the explainers teach the mechanism the papers introduced; a paper card's 'go deeper' link and the explainer's 'the original paper' link are natural two-way pairs.
Scaling Laws (the planned flagship slider widget) ↔ cost-latency/ (tokens.html, latency.html, caching-batching.html): same cost-per-token economics told as history vs. as practice.
Chain-of-Thought + DeepSeek-R1 ↔ agents/ (planning.html, agent-loop.html) and cost-latency: 'buy quality with tokens' is already the explainers' framing — cite the CoT paper card from those articles.

CLIP (embeddings/shared space) ↔ grounding/ series (grounding-your-data.html, graph-rag.html) and wiki/entities/vector-db.md, rag-pipeline.md: the RAG explainers can link CLIP as 'where embeddings came from'.
InstructGPT/RLHF + Constitutional AI ↔ evaluation/ (what-makes-ai-good.html, llm-as-judge.html) and grounding/hallucinations-safety.html: alignment-as-product-layer is the shared thesis.
MoE (Switch Transformers) + LoRA ↔ cost-latency/ and any future fine-tuning explainer: the 'how frontier models scale affordably' cost story.

Mechanical/site-level: both sites live on the same origin (navsha.github.io/<repo>/), so cross-links are plain /ml-papers/ and /interactive-explainers/ paths — no CORS/domain work; add an ml-papers card or 'latest' entry on the explainers homepage and a reciprocal 'want the practice, not the history?' banner on ml-papers.

Editorial tooling: interactive-explainers' lint.py watchlist + wiki/entities could gain paper entities (transformer, scaling-laws, rlhf) whose canonical page lives on ml-papers — turning the wiki's orphan-concept check into a cross-site link planner.

ml-papers README already poses the decision this enables: 'standalone site vs. new topic in interactive-explainers' — cross-linking is how a standalone site keeps the audience benefits of being a topic.
verdictOnSharedInfra: Copy the infrastructure almost wholesale; diverge on IA, visual identity, and one process detail. COPY: (1) the zero-build stack — plain HTML/CSS/JS with inline per-page scripts is proven at 42 pages here and ml-papers is ~15–20 pages max; a bundler would add cost with no benefit, and the Tier-1 widgets planned in VISUALIZATIONS.md (scaling-laws slider, attention hover, CoT side-by-side, denoising scrubber) are all achievable with canvas + vanilla JS exactly like the explainers' widgets; (2) GitHub Pages legacy branch deploy from the default branch root with relative internal links (works under the /ml-papers/ subpath), custom 404.html, hand-written sitemap.xml + robots.txt, https enforced — but deploy from `main`, ml-papers' existing default, not master; (3) the stdlib-Python scripts pattern — port check_links.py and inject_meta.py nearly verbatim (they're root-relative and generic); (4) the governance-docs trio (CLAUDE.md/PRODUCT.md/DESIGN.md) and the design-token CSS architecture, including the accent-quad pattern remapped from 8 topics to the papers' eras or tiers (foundations / LLM core / making-useful / beyond-text / builders); (5) the accessibility floor (WCAG AA, reduced-motion, keyboard widgets) and the meta/OG-on-every-page discipline. DIVERGE: (1) IA — do NOT clone hub-and-spoke series/parts; ml-papers' spine is a single chronological timeline (2012→2025) with tiered depth per paper (Tier-1 widget pages, Tier-2 scrollytelling, Tier-3 cards), so the landing page IS the primary artifact, closer to one Pudding-style piece than a publication of series; breadcrumbs and 'Part N of M' don't map — timeline position and era do. (2) Visual identity — fork the token structure but not the palette/fonts: PRODUCT.md's system (warm paper, Newsreader, rap epigraphs) is the explainers' brand; ml-papers should read as a sibling, not a clone, and scrollytelling with pinned visuals needs its own layout tokens (full-bleed pinned figure + scrolling text column vs. the explainers' single 760px column). (3) Skip the wiki/ planning layer and lint watchlist initially — it earns its keep for a growing 30+ article publication, not a fixed corpus of 14. (4) Fix the two known papercuts rather than inheriting them: replace manual ?v=N cache busting with a single documented bump location (or accept it — with ~15 files it's tolerable, but note CLAUDE.md already drifted from v=10 vs actual v=12), and add one tiny GitHub Action running scripts/check_links.py on push — the explainers get away with zero CI by convention, but a 5-line workflow is free insurance. (5) Use a separate GA property (or none), not the explainers' G-E8RQLQXRWH. Net: same infra philosophy — static files, branch deploy, stdlib scripts, token CSS, docs-as-guardrails — different information architecture and skin.
